observed: The repository contained a deterministic effective-queue reconciler but no repository-level workflow that discovered all append-only queue updates and failed closed on malformed identity or unresolved conflicts.
The new generator, deterministic tests, and GitHub Actions workflow were committed to the default branch.
inferred: A read-only generated queue artifact reduces stale work selection while preserving canonical and append-only source history.
proposed: Use the generated effective queue as the first work-selection view for all teams after the workflow has produced passing evidence.
superseded: Manual queue reconciliation without a repository-level discovery and conflict gate.
unresolved: No workflow run was visible through the available commit-run lookup at evidence creation time, so CI passage is not claimed.
Historical update files may still expose malformed identity, ambiguous chronology, or same-time material conflicts when the workflow runs.

acceptance_contract: discover every JSON update below operations/queue-updates
preserve ACTIVE_QUEUE.json and update files unchanged
fail closed on conflict, malformed update identity, empty update ledger, or missing artifact
upload the derived queue and exact source commit as a bounded CI artifact

verification.source_persistence: GitHub create-file operations returned commit SHAs for generator, tests, and workflow.
verification.workflow_lookup: No workflow run was returned for commit 0d624c653faf32036817d714e82d091bdab32e32 at inspection time.
verification.claim_limit: Committed test coverage is evidence of an executable contract, not evidence that GitHub Actions passed.

privacy_boundary: Public repository operations metadata and abstract project-state claims only. No raw chat, health, pet-health, credentials, personal identifiers, or unreviewed uploads.
falsification_route: A failing workflow, absent generated artifact, silent conflict acceptance, source mutation, or successful malformed update would falsify the gate claim.
